title: Resume Architecture, Evidence Statements, and Machine-Robust Delivery · Claim Audit: Resume Verification
summary: A resume uses decorative columns, keyword blocks, vague adjectives, unsupported impact numbers, and identical content for every role. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resume verification increment in truthful role-targeted resume system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering.
Produce readable semantic source, plain-text, and appropriately generated PDF variants that survive selection, extraction, links, zoom, and reflow checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Resume Verification · Theory Resume Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Resume Verification contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Resume Verification · Theory Resume Verification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering. For Predict Resume Verification · Theory Resume Verification, keep Resume Verification connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Resume Verification condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: EEOC Employment Discrimination and AI for Workers — Controls awareness that automated recruiting, screening, assessment, promotion, and pay tools can implicate protected rights and accommodation needs.

hints: For Predict Resume Verification · Theory Resume Verification, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Resume Verification precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

For Predict Resume Verification · Theory Resume Verification, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Small inconsistencies are harmless because reviewers infer intent.

options.text: Small inconsistencies are harmless because reviewers infer intent. That claim fails the predict resume verification evidence boundary for Resume Verification.

For predict resume verification, apply Resume Verification without its stated preconditions, omit the truthful role-targeted resume system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Resume Verification for truthful role-targeted resume system.
Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Resume Verification while producing truthful role-targeted resume system.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering. A defensible solution applies Resume Verification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to truthful role-targeted resume system.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-resume-verification — Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-5
focus: career-resume-verification
decision-evidence: Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Small inconsistencies are harmless because reviewers infer intent. This fails the Resume Verification contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: EEOC Employment Discrimination and AI for Workers (Worker resource published 2024; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls awareness that automated recruiting, screening, assessment, promotion, and pay tools can implicate protected rights and accommodation needs.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering.
